SQL example 0.
Goal: teach how to parse input with a loop.
The example uses a table and a INTEGER value.
```sql
SELECT user_id, SUM(amount) AS total_0
FROM payments
WHERE amount >= 0
GROUP BY user_id
ORDER BY total_0 DESC;
```
-- Read the code from top to bottom.
The function receives data, skips invalid values, and returns one clear result.
A common mistake in this topic is missing index.
Check the empty input case before trusting the code.
Keep names descriptive, keep steps small, and test one behavior at a time.

The example uses a view and a BOOLEAN value.
```sql
SELECT user_id, SUM(amount) AS total_0
FROM payments
WHERE amount >= 0
GROUP BY user_id
ORDER BY total_0 DESC;
```
-- Read the code from top to bottom.
The function receives data, skips invalid values, and returns one clear result.
A common mistake in this topic is missing index.
Check the empty input case before trusting the code.
Keep names descriptive, keep steps small, and test one behavior at a time.
